title: Lesson 02: Everyday Objects and Noun Gender
description: Learn to identify everyday items using basic nouns, demonstratives, and understand Pashto's grammatical gender.

content.heading: The Logic of Things
lines: To say 'This is [Object]', we start with 'Da' [Dah] (This), followed by the noun, and end with the verb 'is'.
Crucially, Pashto nouns are gendered. If the object is masculine, 'is' translates to 'day' [day]. If feminine, it translates to 'da' [dah].

content.heading: Building Sentences
lines: 1. This is a book: Da ketab day. [Dah keh-taab day]
2. This is tea: Da chay day. [Dah ch-eye day]
3. This is bread: Da dod-day da. [Dah doad-day dah]
4. This is water: Da oba da. [Dah oh-ba dah]

content.text: Grammar Hack: Most nouns ending in a consonant or 'ay' are masculine, while those ending in an 'a' sound are often feminine.

content.question: How would you correctly say 'This is water'?
content.explanation: Because 'oba' (water) is a feminine noun, the verb 'to be' at the end of the sentence must be the feminine 'da'.
